page: Amazon Web Services x8g.medium is a memory-optimized server instance powered by an ARM64-based AWS Graviton processor. It features a single dedicated physical core with one vCPU utilizing the AWS Neoverse-V2 architecture running at 2.8 GHz. The processor includes 64 KB of L1d cache, 2 MB of L2 cache, and 36 MB of L3 cache, with hardware support for AES cryptography. This instance is equipped with 16.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 16.0 GB per vCPU. It does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, and operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor with a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks reflect the single-core nature of this instance. Single-core CPU performance is average, with a stress-ng single-core score of 1694.19 and a Geekbench single-core score of 1740.0. Multi-core benchmarks, such as stress-ng multi-core and Geekbench multi-core, show no scaling due to the single-vCPU limitation. Memory performance is mixed; the PassMark memory score is average at 2137.2, but large-block uncached read bandwidth is weak at 28.26 GB/sec, and write bandwidth is poor at 11.33 GB/sec. Single-threaded bzip3 decompression is strong at 14.31 GB/sec, while database operations and Redis SET operations show weaker throughput.

The primary tradeoff of the x8g.medium is its high memory allocation relative to its single-core compute capacity. This makes it highly cost-efficient for memory-intensive workloads that do not require parallel processing or high multi-core throughput. It is well-suited for lightweight database caching, single-threaded data processing, memory-bound utility scripts, and small-scale web serving where memory capacity is the primary bottleneck rather than raw compute power.
description: Amazon Web Services x8g.medium is a memory-optimized instance powered by a single-core ARM64 AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 processor running at 2.8 GHz. Built on the Nitro hypervisor, it features 16.0 GB of RAM, providing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 16:1. The instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, and has a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps. Benchmark data shows average single-core performance, with a Geekbench single-core score of 1740.0 and strong single-threaded bzip3 decompression at 14.31 GB/sec, though multi-core and memory write bandwidth metrics are low due to the single vCPU limitation. This hardware profile is qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-heavy, single-threaded workloads. It is best suited for lightweight database caching, memory-bound utility tasks, and single-threaded data processing.
og_description: Amazon Web Services x8g.medium is a memory-optimized ARM64 instance featuring a single dedicated Neoverse-V2 core and 16.0 GB of RAM, designed for single-threaded, memory-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services x8g.medium is a memory-optimized ARM64 instance with 1 vCPU and 16.0 GB of RAM, built on the Nitro hypervisor.
tagline: A single-core memory-optimized ARM64 instance providing high memory density for lightweight database caching and single-threaded data processing workloads.
bullet_points: Single dedicated AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 ARM64 core running at 2.8 GHz
16.0 GB of system memory providing a high 16:1 memory-to-vCPU ratio
Average single-core performance with 1740.0 Geekbench single-core score
No local storage and 1 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
Best suited for lightweight database caching and single-threaded processing
